generated_oml:
# Digital Twin entity
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
  base:desc "Digital twin entity representing the virtual environment."
]

# C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
  base:desc "LiDAR sensor on the vehicle that captures raw 3‑D point cloud data."
  DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARRawPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
  base:desc "Vehicle state sensor that reports speed and steering angle."
  DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateRawData
]

# C4: Data transmitted from physical sensors
instance LiDARRawPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
  DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensingComponent
]

instance VehicleStateRawData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
  DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensingComponent
]

# C2: Acting components
instance MotorActingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
  base:desc "Electric motor actuator controlling vehicle acceleration."
]

instance SteeringActingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
  base:desc "Steering actuator managing vehicle direction."
]

instance ServoActingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
  base:desc "Servo actuator for fine‑tuned control surface actuation."
]

# C10: Models/Data (translated data streams)
instance LiDARProcessedPointCloud : DTDFVocab:Data [
  base:desc "Serialized LiDAR point clouds after 2‑D clustering and tracking."
  DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARProcessingEnabler, DataMonitoringEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler
  DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARRawPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateMessage : DTDFVocab:Data [
  base:desc "Pre‑processed vehicle state message with unit conversions."
  DTDFVocab:inputTo StateDataTranslationEnabler, DataMonitoringEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler
  DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateRawData
]

# C11: Enablers
instance LiDARProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
  base:desc "PointCloudClustering & Tracking component converting raw LiDAR data into 2‑D clusters."
  DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance StateDataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
  base:desc "Data translation component that serialises vehicle state data and performs unit conversions."
  DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
  base:desc "Facilitates real‑time visualisation of vehicle state and detected object tracks."
  DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ComputationOffloadingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
  base:desc "Delegates ACC logic (lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, speed command transmission) to a cloud server."
  DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
  base:desc "Enables remote operators to control ACC, set target speeds, and issue emergency braking via the UI."
  DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

# C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
  base:desc "Provides real‑time vehicle state visualisation, projected paths, detected object tracks, and performance reporting."
  DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateVisualizationInsight, ObjectTracksVisualizationInsight,
                    GeneratePerformanceReportAction, SavePerformanceReportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
  base:desc "Implements computation offloading for in‑vehicle safety logic."
  DTDFVocab:provides TransmitSpeedCommandAction, EnableACCAction,
                    DisableACCAction, TriggerEmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
  base:desc "Allows operators to remotely manipulate ACC settings, set target speeds, and trigger emergency braking."
  DTDFVocab:provides SetTargetSpeedAction, EnableACCAction,
                    DisableACCAction, IssueEmergencyBrakeAction,
                    OperatorCommandMonitoringInsight
]

# C17: Insights
instance VehicleStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
  base:desc "Real‑time visualisation of vehicle speed, steering angle, and projected driving path."
]

instance ObjectTracksVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
  base:desc "Tracks of detected objects shown in the user interface."
]

instance OperatorCommandMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
  base:desc "Monitors and logs operator remote commands and system responses."
]

# C17: Actions
instance GeneratePerformanceReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Generates a performance report from collected driving data."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SavePerformanceReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Saves the current performance report to local storage."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance TransmitSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Transmits desired speed commands to vehicle actuators."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Enables Adaptive Cruise Control on the vehicle."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Disables Adaptive Cruise Control on the vehicle."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance TriggerEmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Triggers an emergency braking command via the cloud server."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SetTargetSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Sets a target speed for the vehicle."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance IssueEmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
  base:desc "Issues an emergency braking command from the operator."
  DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

# C5: Environments
instance PhysicalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
  base:contains LiDARSensingComponent, VehicleStateSensingComponent,
                MotorActingComponent, SteeringActingComponent,
                ServoActingComponent, LiDARProcessingEnabler,
                StateDataTranslationEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler,
                RemoteManagementEnabler
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
  base:contains LiDARRawPointCloudData, VehicleStateRawData,
                LiDARProcessedPointCloud, VehicleStateMessage,
                DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService,
                RemoteManagementService, VehicleStateVisualizationInsight,
                ObjectTracksVisualizationInsight, OperatorCommandMonitoringInsight,
                GeneratePerformanceReportAction, SavePerformanceReportAction,
                TransmitSpeedCommandAction, EnableACCAction,
                DisableACCAction, TriggerEmergencyBrakeAction,
                SetTargetSpeedAction, IssueEmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance UserEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
  base:contains UserInterfaceComponent
]

# Functional component for user interface
instance UserInterfaceComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
  base:desc "Web‑based user interface that displays real‑time data, allows remote control, and manages performance reports."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands originate from the User Interface (Control Panel) and include enabling/disabling adaptive cruise control (ACC), adjusting target speed, or issuing an emergency brake. These commands are transmitted to the Actuation FE, which converts desired speed values into motor‑voltage signals and steering commands for the servo. The actuation data are then passed through the data‑translation component, which serialises and aligns them before the command is sent over the Wi‑Fi/UDP link to the physical actuation hardware. Feedback mechanisms, conflict resolution logic and command‑execution confirmation are not described in the provided documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with a temporal granularity of 100 ms, matching the required clock‑synchronisation accuracy mandated by safety‑critical vehicular standards and constrained by hardware limits. Devices share a unified notion of time to enable computation offloading, with inter‑component synchronization maintained within a 100 ms window to meet latency and timing requirements."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration aligns with ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping software components to functional entities and implementing each FE as an individual ROS 2 package containing one or more ROS 2 nodes. The architecture pattern is entity‑based, with data management and control mechanisms grouped under the Digital Twin Entity, physical device functionalities under the Physical Twin Entity, and user‑facing components under the User Entity. Resource management is achieved through ROS 2’s node and package mechanisms, and system‑wide coordination is handled via inter‑entity messaging and shared time‑stamped data streams."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical network uses ROS 2 for intra‑component messaging (implying DDS as the underlying transport) and relies on a Wi‑Fi network for inter‑entity communication via UDP. The system must maintain a network latency of no more than 100 ms between the Digital Twin and the Physical Twin Entity. Bandwidth, reliability, redundancy and security protocols (e.g., TLS, authentication) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The system is deployed across multiple hardware platforms: the Point‑Cloud Clustering & Actuation components reside on the Physical Twin Entity, while data‑collecting, reporting, and storage components run on a separate device external to the vehicle. Ego‑vehicle state data are stored on this external storage device. Specific hosting environments (cloud, edge, on‑premise), computational or storage capacities, scalability mechanisms, deployment models, and availability guarantees are not specified in the provided documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 Part 2 reference architecture specifies an entity‑based framework for Digital Twin development in manufacturing.  It defines four core entities: the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) which represents the physical asset; the Data Collection and Device Control (DDC) Entity responsible for ingesting sensor streams and actuating the OME; the Digital Twin (DT) Entity that holds the virtual model and analytics; and the User Entity that offers monitoring and command interfaces.  Each entity is decomposed into Functional Entities (FEs) which encapsulate modular tasks such as data transformation, user‑interface rendering, or device control.  The standard references OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) as a concrete implementation example for communication, but does not prescribe any proprietary stack.  It also requires that any deployed DT framework be capable of exchanging data, model state, and control commands in compliance with the ISO 23247 data‑exchange specifications."
]
